title: 11 Best Natural Herbal Oils for Dry Lips Healing

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Vitamins a c e in SYMPHYTUM OFFICINALE are good for dry lips because they help to lock in moisture, soothe and protect dry skin, and promote collagen production, which keeps lips healthy, smooth and hydrated.
Anthraquinones are good for dry lips because they help to lock in moisture and soothe irritated skin, promoting the healing and softening of chapped lips.
Aloin is good for dry lips because its soothing properties help to lock in moisture and calm inflammation, making it a natural remedy to heal and protect dry, chapped lips.

Flavonoids, also known as comfrey, help to heal and soothe dry lips due to their powerful antioxidant properties which reduce inflammation and promote collagen production, making them a natural remedy for dry, cracked, and irritated lip skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis essential oil is a natural remedy for dry lips. It's extracted from the aloe vera plant, which is known for its moisturizing properties. This oil helps to lock in moisture and soothe dry, chapped lips. The main compound in aloe barbadensis, called aloin, has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and swelling. When applied to dry lips, aloe barbadensis essential oil creates a barrier that protects the skin from the environment, preventing further dryness and irritation. This oil also has antiseptic properties, which help to prevent infections and promote healing of damaged skin. As a result, aloe barbadensis essential oil is effective in hydrating and soothing dry, cracked lips, making it a popular natural remedy for lip care.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Symphytum officinale herbal essential oils that help with dry lips, start by gathering the necessary ingredients, including Symphytum officinale roots, stems, or leaves, a carrier oil such as coconut or jojoba oil, and a clean glass jar. Begin by drying the Symphytum officinale plant material in a low-temperature oven or by air-drying it for several days, then chop or grind it into small pieces. Next, combine the dried plant material with the carrier oil in a ratio of about 1 part plant material to 4 parts oil, and place the mixture in a clean glass jar. Seal the jar and let it steep in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the oil extract from the plant material. After the steeping period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. The resulting herbal essential oil can be stored in a cool, dark place and used as a natural remedy for dry lips by applying a small amount to the affected area with a cotton swab.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal essential oils for dry lips

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for dry lips because it helps to protect and soothe them by reducing inflammation and fighting free radicals that cause damage.
Flavonoids help to soothe and heal dry lips by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow to the affected area, which in turn helps to repair and regenerate the damaged skin tissue.
Tannins, also known as comfrey, are good for dry lips because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and protect dry, chapped skin, reducing irritation and promoting healing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis essential oil is a natural remedy for dry lips. This oil is extracted from the Calendula flower, which has been used for centuries to heal skin issues. It's rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm dry, chapped lips. The oil's moisturizing properties lock in moisture, reducing the dryness and cracking of the skin. It also has antimicrobial properties that help to prevent infection, making it a great solution for lips that are prone to cuts or cracks. When applied to dry lips, Calendula officinalis essential oil helps to create a barrier that protects the skin from the elements, allowing it to heal naturally. The oil's gentle and non-irritating properties make it safe to use on sensitive skin, making it a great option for people with dry or chapped lips.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Symphytum officinale herbal essential oil that helps with dry lips, you need to gather a few ingredients and some basic equipment. First, harvest fresh Symphytum officinale leaves and flowers from a trusted source or grow them in your garden. Next, dry the plant material using a food dehydrator or by tying it in small bunches and hanging it upside down in a warm, dry place. Once the plant material is completely dry, you can proceed to make the essential oil. To do this, place the dried plant material in a clean glass jar and cover it with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the oil infuse into the plant material. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. Your Symphytum officinale herbal essential oil is now ready to use as a natural remedy for dry lips. Simply apply a few drops of the oil to your lips and massage it in for soothing relief.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal essential oils for dry lips

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita essential oil, also known as peppermint oil, is good for dry lips because of its moisturizing properties. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Symphytum officinale herbal essential oils that help with dry lips, you'll need to start by harvesting the plant, also known as comfrey, in its flowering stage. Harvest the leaves and flowers, and dry them completely to remove excess moisture. You can do this by tying them in small bunches and hanging them upside down in a warm, dry, dark place. Once the herbs are dry, you can start making your oil by placing a cup of the dried herbs in a clean glass jar and covering them with a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil. Store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the herbs release their goodness into the oil. After the sitting period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, and discard the solids. Your Symphytum officinale herbal essential oil is now ready to use, and you can apply it to your dry lips to help soothe and moisturize them.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil, also known as Tea Tree oil, is a natural remedy for dry lips. The oil has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm dry, cracked lips. The oil also has antiseptic properties, which can help to prevent infection and promote healing of the skin. Additionally, Tea Tree oil can help to moisturize and hydrate the skin, reducing dryness and flakiness. When applied to dry lips, it can provide instant relief and protection from the elements, promoting healthy and smooth lips. The oil's antifungal properties can also help to combat fungal infections that may be contributing to the dryness of the lips.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Symphytum officinale herbal essential oils that help with dry lips, start by harvesting fresh Symphytum officinale leaves in the summer months when the plant is in full bloom. Clean and dry the leaves thoroughly to prevent any contamination. Next, chop the leaves into small pieces and fill a glass jar about 2/3 full. Cover the leaves with a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil and seal the jar. Allow the mixture to steep in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily to help the oil extract from the leaves. After the steeping process, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids. Finally, transfer the extracted oil to a glass bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. The resulting Symphytum officinale herbal essential oil can be used topically to soothe and moisturize dry lips, providing relief from discomfort and helping to promote healthy skin.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal essential oils for dry lips

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oil, also known as rosemary oil, is often used to help with dry and chapped lips. This is because it has moisturizing properties that can help lock in moisture and soothe dry skin. Rosemary oil has antibacterial properties, which can help prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause infections on the lips. It also has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling. When applied to dry lips, rosemary oil can help calm irritation and promote healing. Additionally, rosemary oil contains antioxidants that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dryness and aging. When used in combination with a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, rosemary oil can be a effective remedy for dry and chapped lips.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Symphytum officinale herbal essential oil that helps with dry lips, start by drying the leaves and flowers of the Symphytum officinale plant, also known as comfrey. You can do this by tying the leaves and flowers in small bunches and hanging them upside down in a warm, dry place. Once they are completely dry, you can use a solvent like jojoba oil or grapeseed oil to extract the essential oils from the plant material. To do this, place the dried leaves and flowers in a clean glass jar and cover them with the solvent. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the solvent extract the oils from the plant material. After the extraction period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the plant material. The resulting liquid is the Symphytum officinale herbal essential oil, which can be used to help soothe and moisturize dry lips.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal essential oils for dry lips

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis essential oil, also known as lemon balm oil, has soothing properties that can help with dry lips. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Symphytum officinale herbal essential oils for dry lips, start by gathering fresh Symphytum officinale leaves and flowers, also known as comfrey leaves and flowers. Clean and dry the herbs thoroughly, then place them in a clean glass jar. Next, add a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or jojoba oil to cover the herbs completely, making sure the herbs are fully submerged. Seal the jar and place it in a cool, dark place, allowing the mixture to steep for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally. After the steeping period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. To extract the remaining oil, you can use a coffee filter or a centrifuge to press the oil from the solids. Finally, transfer the extracted oil to a clean glass bottle and store it in a cool, dark place, allowing it to mature for a few weeks before using it on dry lips.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oil, also known as English lavender oil, is a natural remedy for dry lips. It has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm dry, chapped lips. When applied to the lips, it can reduce redness and swelling, making them look healthier and more comfortable. The oil also has moisturizing properties that help lock in moisture and prevent dryness. This is because it contains compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which are known for their hydrating and protective properties. Additionally, the antibacterial properties of English lavender oil can help prevent infections that can cause dry lips to become even drier and more painful. As a result, using English lavender oil on dry lips can provide quick relief and help to heal them naturally.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Symphytum officinale herbal essential oils that help with dry lips, you need a few ingredients and some basic equipment. First, you'll need fresh or dried Symphytum officinale leaves and flowers, which are also known as comfrey. You can grow your own comfrey plant or purchase it from a local herbal store. Next, you'll need a clean glass jar with a lid, a carrier oil like olive or jojoba oil, and a solvent like ethanol or glycerin. To make the oil, combine 1 part dried comfrey leaves and flowers with 2 parts carrier oil in the glass jar. Secure the lid and let the mixture steep in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally. After the steeping period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. If you want to make a more concentrated oil, you can repeat the steeping process a few more times. Finally, add a few drops of glycerin or ethanol to the oil to help preserve it and improve its texture. Your comfrey oil is now ready to use on dry lips, helping to soothe and moisturize them.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oils for dry lips

remedies.remedy_desc: Cocos nucifera, also known as coconut oil, is a natural remedy for dry lips. The fatty acids in coconut oil, like lauric acid and oleic acid, help to moisturize and nourish dry skin. When applied to the lips, these fatty acids form a protective barrier that locks in moisture and prevents dryness. Coconut oil also contains antioxidants, which fight off free radicals that can cause further dryness and irritation. As a result, coconut oil helps to soothe and calm dry, chapped lips, reducing discomfort and making them feel smoother and softer. The antibacterial properties of coconut oil can also help to prevent the spread of infection, making it a safe and effective solution for dry, cracked lips.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Symphytum officinale herbal essential oil that helps with dry lips, you'll need fresh Symphytum officinale leaves and stems, a clean glass jar with a lid, a carrier oil like jojoba or sweet almond oil, and a steam distillation apparatus or a home distillation kit. First, harvest fresh Symphytum officinale leaves and stems, making sure to identify the plant correctly and only pick what's needed. Next, chop the plant material into small pieces and place it in the distillation apparatus or home distillation kit. Then, add water to the apparatus and heat it until steam is produced, causing the plant's essential oils to be released and collected in a separate container. Once the distillation process is complete, carefully collect the essential oil and mix it with a carrier oil in a ratio of about 1-3% essential oil to 97-99% carrier oil. This blend can be used to create a balm or salve for dry lips, providing relief and hydration. Finally, store the finished product in an airtight container and use it as needed to soothe and protect dry, chapped lips.
remedies.image_alt: Cocos nucifera herbal essential oils for dry lips

When applied to dry lips, this oil can help to lock in moisture and provide long-lasting hydration, leaving lips feeling soft and smooth. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea essential oil has antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause infections and further irritation.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Symphytum officinale herbal essential oils that help with dry lips, you'll need a few simple ingredients and some basic equipment. First, you'll need to harvest the Symphytum officinale plant, also known as comfrey, which has anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that help soothe dry lips. Once you have the plant, dry the leaves and flowers thoroughly in a cool, dark place or using a dehydrator to remove excess moisture. Next, chop the dried plant material into small pieces and mix it with a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a ratio of about 1 part plant material to 5 parts oil. Transfer the mixture to a glass jar and place it in a warm, dark place, allowing the plant's properties to infuse into the oil over a period of 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally to help the mixture blend together. Finally, after the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the plant material from the oil, and discard the solids. remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale essential oil, also known as comfrey oil, is a natural remedy for dry lips. It has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling. Comfrey oil is rich in antioxidants, which protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, helping to lock in moisture and hydrate dry lips. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Symphytum officinale herbal essential oil, also known as comfrey oil, that helps with dry lips, you will need comfrey leaves and flowers, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and a clean glass jar with a lid. First, harvest comfrey leaves and flowers, making sure they are fresh and free of any contamination. Then, dry the comfrey leaves and flowers by placing them in a single layer on a paper towel or clean cloth, allowing them to air dry completely. Once dry, chop the comfrey leaves and flowers into small pieces and fill the glass jar about half full. Next, pour the carrier oil over the comfrey pieces, making sure they are completely covered. Secure the lid on the jar and place it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the oil extract from the comfrey. After the extraction period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Your comfrey oil is now ready to use as a natural remedy for dry lips.

intro_desc: Dry, chapped lips can be painful and annoying. They can crack and bleed, making it hard to eat, speak, or even smile. Fixing this issue is important because it affects your daily life and overall comfort. If you have dry, chapped lips, you may avoid eating certain foods, avoid social interactions, or even feel self-conscious about your appearance.  Herbal essential oils can be a great solution to this problem. Aloe barbadensis, also known as aloe vera, is a popular remedy for dry skin, including lips. It contains compounds that soothe and moisturize the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. When used as an essential oil, aloe vera can be easily absorbed into the skin, providing long-lasting relief.  Another herb that can help with dry lips is Calendula officinalis, also known as marigold. Calendula has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help calm and protect dry, chapped skin. When applied to the lips, calendula essential oil can reduce redness, swelling, and irritation, promoting healthy skin.  Using herbal essential oils like aloe vera and calendula can improve your life by providing quick and effective relief from dry, chapped lips. By incorporating these oils into your skincare routine, you can enjoy smoother, healthier-looking lips that feel comfortable and pain-free. This can boost your confidence, make social interactions easier, and allow you to enjoy your favorite foods without discomfort.
supplementary_best: The best combination of herbal essential oils to remedy dry lips is Aloe barbadensis, Calendula officinalis, and Melaleuca alternifolia. Aloe barbadensis provides moisture and soothes dry, cracked lips, while Calendula officinalis has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and irritation. Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree oil, has antimicrobial properties that help prevent infection and promote healing. This combination works well because it addresses all aspects of dry lips: hydration, inflammation, and infection prevention. The aloe vera in Aloe barbadensis helps lock in moisture, while the calendula oil reduces inflammation and promotes healing. The tea tree oil then prevents infection and speeds up the healing process. When used together, these essential oils create a powerful blend that effectively remedies dry lips.
supplementary_avoid: If you have dry lips, it's a good idea to avoid using essential oils that can irritate or dry out your skin further. Some oils to be careful with include Bergamot (Citrus aurantium bergamia) oil, which can cause skin photosensitivity and make dry lips even drier. Another oil to avoid is Cinnamon (Cinnamomum verum) oil, as it can be too harsh and strip your skin of its natural moisture. Also, avoid using Frankincense (Boswellia serrata) oil, as it can be drying and irritating to dry skin. Sandalwood (Santalum album) oil is another one to be cautious with, as it can be too thick and greasy, which can clog pores and make dry lips feel worse. Finally, avoid using Tea Tree (Cananga odorata) oil, as it can be too strong and drying, especially on sensitive areas like the lips.
supplementary_other_remedies: Honey is a natural remedy that can help with dry lips. It has moisturizing properties that lock in moisture, soothing and softening the lips. Honey is also a natural humectant, which means it attracts water from the air and helps retain it, keeping the lips hydrated for a longer time. When applied to dry lips, honey forms a protective barrier that helps shield the lips from the wind, cold weather, and other environmental factors that can dry them out.  Aloe vera gel is another natural remedy that can provide relief to dry lips. It is rich in vitamins and minerals, particularly vitamin E, which is known for its ability to nourish and moisturize the skin. Aloe vera gel also has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm irritated lips. When applied to dry lips, aloe vera gel helps to repair and restore the natural moisture balance, leaving the lips feeling smooth and soft.  Coconut oil is a natural moisturizer that can help to hydrate and nourish dry lips. It contains fatty acids that help to lock in moisture and soothe dry, chapped skin. Coconut oil also has antibacterial properties that can help prevent infections and promote healing. When applied to dry lips, coconut oil helps to repair and restore the natural moisture balance, leaving the lips feeling smooth and soft.  Cocoa butter is a natural moisturizer that can help to hydrate and nourish dry lips. It contains fatty acids that help to lock in moisture and soothe dry, chapped skin. Cocoa butter also has antioxidants that can help protect the lips from environmental stressors and promote healing. When applied to dry lips, cocoa butter helps to repair and restore the natural moisture balance, leaving the lips feeling smooth and soft.  Lanolin is a natural wax that is often used to soothe and protect dry, chapped skin, including dry lips. It creates a barrier on the surface of the lips that helps to lock in moisture and protect the lips from the elements. Lanolin is also a natural emollient that helps to smooth and soften the lips, leaving them feeling comfortable and hydrated.  Ghee, or clarified butter, is a natural moisturizer that can help to hydrate and nourish dry lips. It contains fatty acids that help to lock in moisture and soothe dry, chapped skin. Ghee also has antioxidant properties that can help protect the lips from environmental stressors and promote healing. When applied to dry lips, ghee helps to repair and restore the natural moisture balance, leaving the lips feeling smooth and soft.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with dry lips may also experience other health issues that can be treated with medicinal herbs. One common problem is dry mouth, also known as xerostomia. This occurs when the body produces less saliva, leading to dehydration and further dryness in the mouth and lips. Aloe vera, an herb with soothing and hydrating properties, can be used to treat dry mouth by promoting saliva production and moisturizing the oral tissues.  Another issue people with dry lips may face is skin irritation and eczema. These conditions often cause redness, itching, and inflammation on the skin, including the lips. Calendula, a herb with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, can be used to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.  People with dry lips may also experience digestive issues, such as constipation and acid reflux. These conditions can be linked to dehydration, which is a common cause of dry lips. Dandelion root, a herb with diuretic and digestive properties, can help to stimulate digestion and relieve constipation, reducing the risk of acid reflux and other digestive problems.  In addition, people with dry lips may be more susceptible to cold sores and fever blisters, caused by the herpes simplex virus. Echinacea, a herb with antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, can be used to boost the immune system and reduce the severity of cold sore outbreaks.  Finally, people with dry lips may experience respiratory issues, such as coughs and sore throats, often caused by dehydration and poor air quality. Thyme, a herb with antimicrobial and expectorant properties, can be used to soothe and calm the respiratory system, reducing coughs and sore throats.
